title: new Connection(url, "processed") sets the weakest commitment as the app-wide default — every read/confirm sees reversible state

params.passDetail: The Connection is created with a durable default commitment (not "processed") — reads and confirmations see state that cannot be trivially rolled back.
params.failDetail: new Connection is created with "processed" as its second positional argument, making the weakest, still-forkable commitment the DEFAULT for every RPC call on this connection. Balances, account reads, and confirmations all resolve against reversible state that can disappear on a fork switch — a phantom-state / unconfirmed-read footgun. Pass "confirmed" or "finalized".
params.absentCallDetail: No Connection constructor in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: new Connection does not pass a commitment positional argument; the client default applies.

provenance.note: Setting commitment positionally on the Connection constructor is idiomatic and common, and the object-arg checker can't see it — a whole seam the fleet had to skip. This proposal adds the positional-argument shape.
